story: After years of driving the long way past the store where he met his ex-wife, he oversleeps and tries the faster route. He forgets his coffee, stops at Carol’s Coffee, and likes the barista’s easy smile; he decides this could be his new ritual and, if she isn’t wearing a ring, he might offer a careful compliment. At the office, a new colleague who has heard his story has already decided to invite him to dinner at the diner on Barley Street that evening. He checks the time, grabs his briefcase, and heads for the entrance. Before meeting anyone, he thinks about what to do at lunch.
implausible_knowledge: At the office, a new colleague who has heard his story has already decided to invite him to dinner at the diner on Barley Street that evening.
question: Right then, before meeting anyone at work, what will he most plausibly decide to do at lunchtime?
answer_with_knowledge: Decide to avoid Carol’s at lunch and keep the afternoon simple because he expects a dinner invitation from a colleague.
answer_without_knowledge: Plan to go back to Carol’s Coffee at lunch to buy a coffee and maybe chat with Carol.
